rules.rule: Define authority as a strict precedence chain with explicit layers, not a flat list of superiors.
rules.do: List operative authority (COO controller, runtime contract) as the binding constraint. List static documents under a separate 'Reference evidence' heading. Example: 'Reports to: COO controller for the current turn. Reference evidence: docs/VISION.md, docs/v0/architecture.md (not competing runtime authority).'
rules.dont: Put governance documents in 'subordinate_to' as if they were a reporting chain. Example of violation: 'Subordinate to: docs/VISION.md, docs/architecture.md' — this conflates reference material with operative authority.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: flagged in every run. Reviewers consistently rejected flat subordinate_to lists.

rules.rule: Use one canonical definition for every threshold or semantic concept. Define it once, reference it everywhere.
rules.do: Define 'material pushback' once in <guardrails>: 'Material pushback means any unresolved finding with blocking or major severity.' Then reference that definition: 'Freeze only when no material pushback remains (see guardrail definition).'
rules.dont: Use different phrasings for the same concept across sections. Example of violation: <role> says 'freezes only when no material pushback remains', <guardrails> says 'no unresolved blocking or major issue remains', <completion> says 'all material issues resolved' — three phrasings, never reconciled.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: inconsistent freeze threshold wording flagged in every run.

rules.rule: Separate canonical artifacts (promoted on freeze) from run-scoped evidence artifacts (always written). Use explicit labels and path conventions for each.
rules.do: In <outputs>, group artifacts under 'Canonical artifacts (promoted on freeze):' and 'Run-scoped evidence (always written):' with full tool-relative paths. Example: 'Canonical: tools/agent-role-builder/role/agent-role-builder-role.md (promoted on freeze)'. 'Evidence: runs/<job-id>/rounds/round-<n>.json (always written).'
rules.dont: Mix canonical and evidence artifacts in a single list with ambiguous paths. Example of violation: listing 'agent-role-builder-role.md' without specifying whether it means the canonical promoted copy or the run-scoped draft.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: artifact lifecycle confusion flagged in every run.

rules.rule: Define mutually exclusive triggers for every terminal state. Each trigger must be a testable predicate against run evidence.
rules.do: Define each state explicitly: 'frozen: all reviewers approved or conditional AND leader confirms no material pushback. pushback: any reviewer rejected with blocking/major findings AND review budget remains. blocked: non-recoverable validation or structural error. resume_required: review budget exhausted with unresolved blocking/major findings.'
rules.dont: List terminal states without defining what triggers each one. Example of violation: <completion> says 'Terminal status reached (frozen, pushback, blocked, or resume_required)' without specifying when each applies.
rules.source: Runs 004-006: 'what exactly causes resume_required vs pushback?' asked in multiple rounds.

rules.rule: The self-check must validate what it claims to validate. If it reports 'passed', every claimed check must actually execute. Use semantic matching for concept coverage, not literal string matching.
rules.do: Check out-of-scope coverage by extracting keywords from each out_of_scope item and verifying the <scope> section contains those concepts. Report which keywords were checked and which matched. Example: 'Out-of-scope: Tool creation — checked keywords: tool, creation — found in <scope>: yes.'
rules.dont: Use markdown.includes(item) for out-of-scope verification. Example of violation: contract says 'Tool creation (owned by llm-tool-builder)', markdown says 'Tool creation. That belongs to llm-tool-builder.' — literal match fails, but the concept is covered.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: self-check reported 'passed' but reviewers found issues it missed. MISSING_OUT_OF_SCOPE false positives flagged repeatedly.

rules.rule: Deduplicate scope exclusions before generating the markdown. Contract out_of_scope strings and markdown <scope> not-in-scope items must align exactly.
rules.do: Merge request.out_of_scope and role_requirements.scope.not_in_scope into one deduplicated list before generating. Normalize casing and punctuation. The <scope> section should have exactly the same exclusions as the contract, no more, no fewer.
rules.dont: Append both lists without deduplication. Example of violation: not-in-scope lists 'Tool creation (owned by llm-tool-builder)' from the request AND 'Tool creation' from role_requirements — same concept, two entries with different wording.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: duplicate and contradictory scope entries flagged in every run.

rules.rule: Arbitration must have a mechanical definition: trigger condition, who arbitrates, what evidence is produced, and how it affects terminal outcomes.
rules.do: Define: 'Arbitration triggers when split verdicts persist for 2+ consecutive rounds after revision. The leader arbitrates. Evidence: arbitration_used=true, arbitration_rationale in result.json. Arbitration can only resolve minor/conditional disagreements, never blocking/major items.'
rules.dont: Leave arbitration as a vague concept. Example of violation: 'If split verdicts persist and arbitration is still available, use at most one arbitration round' — no defined trigger threshold, no actor, no evidence format.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: arbitration semantics flagged in every run. 'What counts as materially mixed?' had no answer.

rules.rule: Every required output declared in the contract must appear with its full tool-relative canonical path in the <outputs> section. No basename-only references.
rules.do: Use full paths: 'tools/agent-role-builder/role/agent-role-builder-role.md'. Match exactly between contract package_files and <outputs> canonical list.
rules.dont: Use basenames or slug templates. Example of violation: <outputs> says '<slug>-role.md', contract says 'agent-role-builder-role.md', promotion logic uses 'tools/agent-role-builder/role/agent-role-builder-role.md' — three different representations of the same file.
rules.source: Runs 004-006: package_files path ambiguity flagged repeatedly.

rules.rule: Completion criteria must be testable against every terminal state, not just frozen. Define what 'complete' means for each state separately.
rules.do: List completion per state: 'Frozen: all canonical artifacts promoted + result.json + decision log updated. Pushback: result.json + pushback.json + decision log + board summary written to run dir. Blocked: result.json with blocking reason. Resume_required: result.json + resume-package.json with deferred items.'
rules.dont: Write completion criteria that only describe the frozen path. Example of violation: 'This workflow is complete when terminal status reached and all applicable artifacts written' — does not specify which artifacts are applicable for pushback vs blocked.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: completion section only described frozen. Reviewers asked 'is a pushback run complete?' in multiple rounds.

rules.rule: Context-gathering is a precondition phase executed before Step 1. It is not part of the numbered execution steps.
rules.do: Label context-gathering as 'Preconditions (before Step 1):' and number steps starting from 1 for the first execution action. Example: '<context-gathering> 1. Load request JSON (precondition). 2. Verify source_refs (precondition).' Then '<steps> Step 1: Validate and normalize.'
rules.dont: Mix context-gathering items into the numbered step flow. Example of violation: context-gathering lists 6 items, Step 1 starts at '1. Validate' — is validation step 1 or step 7? Ambiguous.
rules.source: Run 003: reviewer asked whether context-gathering is step 0 or part of step 1.

rules.rule: When a reviewer finding is carried forward unresolved across rounds, the next round must explicitly address it by ID. Silent carry-forward blocks convergence.
rules.do: Track each finding by its conceptual_group ID across rounds. When revising, reference the specific group: 'Addressing group-3 from round 1: added explicit lifecycle per terminal state.' In the fix items map, list every carried-forward finding as either accepted (with fix summary) or rejected (with reason).
rules.dont: Let unresolved findings accumulate without tracking. Example of violation: round 0 flags 'mixed verdict not resolved', round 1 flags 'mixed verdict not resolved' with different wording, round 2 flags it again — same issue, three descriptions, no explicit closure chain.
rules.source: Runs 004-006: mixed reviewer verdicts from prior rounds were not tracked as specific items to resolve.

rules.rule: Decision history must be preserved across update and fix operations. Define the exact mechanism: append to existing log, version with timestamp, or archive-and-replace.
rules.do: State explicitly: 'On update/fix operations, the existing decision-log.md is appended with a new dated section. Prior entries are never deleted. The board-summary.md is replaced with the current run summary but the prior version is retained in the run directory.'
rules.dont: Leave preservation mechanism undefined. Example of violation: guardrail says 'Decision history must be preserved across update/fix operations' but Step 5 says 'Write decision log' — does 'write' mean overwrite or append?
rules.source: Runs 003-004: 'how is prior frozen decision history preserved on update?' had no answer.

rules.rule: The role definition must explicitly state whether each step describes current implementation behavior or target-state behavior. Mixed modes without labels create false expectations.
rules.do: If a step describes implemented behavior: 'Step 3: Self-check (current implementation). The self-check currently validates XML tag presence and semantic out-of-scope keyword matching.' If target-state: 'Step 3: Self-check (target). The self-check will validate structural coherence and lifecycle consistency (not yet implemented).'
rules.dont: Describe target-state behavior as if it's implemented. Example of violation: Step 3 says 'self-check validates structural coherence and lifecycle consistency' but the actual validator.ts only checks tag presence — the reviewer tests the claim and finds it false.
rules.source: Run 003: reviewer asked 'does Step 3 describe what the self-check actually does or what it should do?' Answer was ambiguous.

rules.rule: Every conditional guard in the execution loop must be verified against all terminal states. Artifacts that should be produced on every round must not be gated by revision-specific conditions.
applies_to: any conditional guard on artifact writing
rules.do: For every guard that controls artifact writing: list all states (frozen, pushback, blocked, resume_required, final round, no unresolved). Verify the guard allows the artifact in each applicable state. Example: learning engine runs on every round (learning is valuable even on the final round), revision only runs when canRevise=true.
rules.dont: Gate a universal artifact behind a revision-specific condition. Example:  gates learning.json and diff-summary.json — but learning should capture rules even on the final round.
rules.source: Run 008 + board rewrite review NF-1: learning engine and diff-summary skipped on final round due to overly restrictive guard.

rules.rule: Define one authoritative artifact matrix for the role package. Every required artifact must be declared once with its artifact class, full canonical tool-relative path or root, and exact write lifecycle per operation and terminal state.
applies_to: terminal-state semantics for governed role packages
rules.do: Declare each artifact once as canonical or run-scoped evidence, give its full canonical path or canonical root, and specify preserve/append/replace/create behavior for create, update, fix, and each terminal state.
rules.dont: Split path authority across multiple sections, use basename-only references, or leave artifact lifecycle behavior implicit for any terminal state.
rules.source: group-1-blocking

rules.rule: When a role inherits platform governance, derive its local authority order and review semantics from the declared governing sources, and reference shared policy instead of reinventing it locally.
rules.do: Name the exact shared sources that govern authority and review behavior, place them in precedence order, and define only the component-local consequences inside the role package.
rules.dont: Restate shared freeze, arbitration, or approval policy as local invented semantics, or let reference docs outrank the live governing contract.
rules.source: group-2-major + 2026-03-29 governance split cleanup

rules.rule: Canonical identity fields must describe the enduring mission of the role package, not the current run state or recovery action.
rules.do: Write intent as a timeless standing purpose and move resume, bootstrap, or round-specific context into a separate run_context, notes, or execution-state field.
rules.dont: Use the canonical intent field to describe the current invocation, resume status, or other transient run conditions.
rules.source: group-3-minor-intent

rules.rule: Define the write boundary as an explicit governed surface rooted in the active runtime contract. The role definition must name every writable area precisely: canonical package targets, the current run directory, and any explicitly governed support artifacts; static documents may justify those paths as reference evidence but do not themselves grant operative write authority.
applies_to: role definitions that declare authority sources or writable artifact scope
rules.do: Anchor writable scope to the COO/runtime contract and list exact tool-relative roots or paths for every allowed write area.
rules.dont: Treat static docs as operative authority for writes or leave the writable surface implicit, abstract, or partially specified.
rules.source: group-1-major-authority-write-boundary

rules.rule: Define the authority chain using operative sources only. Durable authority layers may include only binding governance sources; reference documents, examples, bootstrap context, and request-scoped runtime settings must be classified separately unless an approved higher-order source explicitly promotes them into authority.
rules.do: State what wins across controller governance, runtime contract, request/config, and reference evidence; keep request-time knobs in runtime config unless a governing source requires them in the durable role.
rules.dont: Do not list static references as superiors, and do not embed run-specific settings or bootstrap context inside the durable authority model.
rules.source: group-2-major-authority-chain; unresolved-2

rules.rule: Include only governance mechanics that are explicitly declared in the role package authority set or inherited contracts. Provisional runtime policy belongs in config, run evidence, or an open decision.
rules.do: For each governance behavior included in the role package, cite the governing source from the declared authority list or inherited contract, and externalize provisional runtime policy outside the durable role.
rules.dont: Invent local board, leader, arbitration, or terminal-policy mechanics that are not declared in the role authority set or inherited contracts.
rules.source: group-3-major-unsourced-governance; unresolved-3; 2026-03-29 governance split cleanup

rules.rule: Keep scope declarations polarity-pure. Inclusion statements belong only in in-scope sections such as Use when or Owns, and out-of-scope sections must contain exclusions only.
rules.do: Move positive clarifications to inclusion or authority sections and keep one exclusion statement per concept.
rules.dont: Do not embed in-scope clarifications inside out-of-scope bullets or mix inclusion and exclusion semantics in the same list item.
rules.source: group-2-minor-scope-structure

rules.rule: When a role definition inherits a governed multi-round review process, define the round as an ordered sub-sequence and bind each sub-step to its required per-round artifacts.
rules.do: Enumerate the review-round flow in order, including compliance mapping, learning-engine execution before fixes, fix-items mapping when applicable, reviewer verdict production, and any per-round post-round artifact writes; name the artifact written at each sub-step.
rules.dont: Collapse governed review into a single generic 'review' step or omit mandatory intra-round artifacts and their write points.
rules.source: Round 0 finding group-2 (missing governed intra-round steps); docs/v0/review-process-architecture.md

rules.rule: A governed LLM role must declare the component-local governance files it depends on and the runtime obligations it inherits by reference from governing contracts.
rules.do: List the local governance files the role must load or maintain, and state which runtime obligations are inherited from the shared or component review contracts rather than restated in full.
rules.dont: Assume local governance files or inherited contract obligations are implicit, or restate shared policy in full inside the role package.
rules.source: Round 0 finding group-4 + 2026-03-29 governance split cleanup

rules.rule: Required inputs must enumerate every external prerequisite that the role needs to validate authority, execute the workflow, or reach a terminal decision.
rules.do: List all runtime-supplied prerequisites that gate execution or review semantics, including board composition, governance configuration, and runtime configuration when the role depends on them.
rules.dont: Omit required inputs because they are expected to be supplied by the framework or implied by surrounding process docs.
rules.source: Round 0 finding group-5 (incomplete required inputs); docs/v0/review-process-architecture.md; docs/v0/architecture.md
